FUTURE ACCOUNTING

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status To Confirm Receipt Of Transfer (Sacramento County)
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Off Calendar

PETITION TO TRANSFER TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY
Off Calendar Nov-07-2017 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Randolph Sharon (via Court Call) for (petitioner/trustee) West America Bank. Heath Langle (via Court Call) for (interested party) IBAR.

The Court noted that Mr. Tomioka is not present for the beneficiary. Mr. Sharon stated that he had not heard from Mr. Tomioka but has received his declaration. The Court asked why the assessment fee has not been paid. Mr. Sharon stated that it should be addressed by the conservator and that if it has not been paid it is because it has not been presented to the trustee. The Court that in regards to fees, fees requested to do the accounting on top of trustee's fees is not the Court's normal practice and that in regards to IBAR's fees $10,000.00 for 14.5 hours of work is unreasonable. Mr. Sharon addressed the accounting fees stating that West America has reduced its fees to 3/4 of 1% as the agreed upon rate, that other banks charge a much greater fee, that no other banks deduct accounting fees from trustee fees, that West America will not continue to act at this fee as well as pay8ing accounting fees and that it is customary to ask for trustee and accounting fees. The Court stated that it is not customary here. Mr. Sharon stated that he can understand it in a probate matter but not a special needs trust where the rate is already cut in half and that they are not willing to have the accounting fees taken out of the trustee fees. Mr. Langle addressed the IBAR fee stating that its function is to make sure the beneficiary remains entitled to benefits, that they guard against duplicate payments and make recommendations to the trustee, that their fee is .25%, that even though there was not a lot of hours IBAR was still involved and that if during another period additional work/hours were needed the fee would be the same. Mr. Sharon asked about the transfer. The Court stated that it is not before the Court and if they want to request a transfer they can petition for that relief. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Continued To Feb-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Armon Cooper for West America Bank.

The Court noted that there are accounting issues that need to be addressed and raised the issue of whether something could be done with the funds to benefit the beneficiary. Mr. Cooper stated that he filed a declaration yesterday, that he will submit e-filed copies to the Probate Examiner today, that the Beneficiary is conserved, that the Court investigation report indicated that the Conservatee is well cared for and that his needs are being met. Mr. Cooper also spoke of the Conservatee's medical condition, stated that the Conservatee can not leave the facility and that the Conservatee is not very responsive. The Court asked Counsel to have the Conservator speak with the Trustee to see if anything can be provided to make the Beneficiary's life better. The accounting petition was continued to February 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for review of the declaration and any information regarding the Beneficiary's well being.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Case Manager And Benefits For Mr. Jefferson
Off Calendar Jan-09-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-05-288189 Conservatorhsip of Terrell Jefferson.

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Ford (Successor Conservator of the Person).

The Court noted that Mr. Cooper was not present. Ms. Rasch stated that there is no resistance on the part of the Trust paying for services, that Paula Bibbs agreed to step back in as Case Manager, that Ms. Bibbs is working with the Social Worker and the LPS Conservator, and explained the difficulty in determining what is appropriate for the Conservatee due to the need to have him in a strict environment because of his extreme behavioral issues. Ms. Rasch also stated that they purchased a TV and DVD palyer for the Conservatee's room, that they have to be careful as to what kind of movies he is watching and that his clothing is adequate. The status hearings were taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Case Manager And Benefits For Mr. Jefferson
Off Calendar Dec-03-2012 Continued To Jan-09-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-09-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following two cases were called together: PTR-05-287126 The Terrell r. Jefferson Special Needs Trust and PCN-05-288189 Conservatorship of Terrell Jefferson.

Appearance: Armon Cooper for West America Bank (Trustee) in the Special Needs Trust case and as Petitioner in the Conservatorship case. Nancy Rasch for Conservator of Person.

The Court questioned why there was nothing done for Mr. Jefferson's benefit when the SNT has over $1,000,000.00 plus additional payments. Mr. Cooper explained that Mr. Jefferson is in Crestwood and that his housing and medical needs are provided by Medi-Cal and that he did not have any other needs and he further reported that he did not know the reason for the lapse of Medi-Cal benefit but it has been resolved by IBAR. Discussion were held with regard to finding services that benefit Mr. Jefferson. Ms. Rasch will talk to her client with hiring a case manager and see what other options are available and she will file a Request for Special Notice in the Special Needs Trust case to get notice of the accounting. Mr. Cooper will file a further response on reducing fees for IBAR. The 4th Account and the Petition for Attorney Fees are taken under submission. Status on case manager and benefits for Mr. Jefferson is set on December 12, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. in both cases.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Chien.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: M. Armon Cooper for West America Bank.

Mr. Cooper stated that there was a probate conservatorship for the estate, that there was an LPS conservatorship as the Conservatee was placed in locked facility and that they agreed to keep the estate conservatorship because of other health care issues. Mr. Cooper also stated that Queen Parks resigned, that Debra Ford was petitioning to be appointed, that Ms. Ford should also serve on the advisory committee and that he would take the steps to do so, and that the Attorney's fees and IBAR's fees had been approved in the previous accountings. The hearing on the petition for resignation set for October 13, 2010 on case number 288189 was continued to October 21, 2010 to be heard with the petition for appointment of Successor
Comservator. The Court approved the Third Account (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
